name: Camera
summary: The Camera class contains methods for creating and manipulating a camera. The camera in SketchUp is the “point of view” from which you look at the model.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.signature: Camera(eye, target, up, perspective = true, fov = 30.0)
constructors.summary: Returns a new camera with eye (where the camera is) and targets (where the camera is looking).
constructors.remarks: Returns a new camera with eye (where the camera is) and targets (where the camera is looking).

methods.name: center_2d
methods.summary: The #center_2d method returns a point with the x and y offset of the camera when it's in two-point perspective or math photo mode.
methods.remarks: The #center_2d method returns a point with the x and y offset of the camera when it's in two-point perspective or math photo mode. When the camera is in two-point perspective and the user pans around, the x and y values will change. These values are in normalized device coordinates, so for instance, the range [-1.0, 1.0] spans the full width or height of the screen. The z value is unused and it is always zero.

methods.name: direction
methods.summary: The #direction method is used to retrieve a Vector3d object in the direction that the Camera is pointing.
methods.remarks: The #direction method is used to retrieve a Vector3d object in the direction that the Camera is pointing.

methods.name: eye
methods.summary: The #eye method is used to retrieve the eye Point3d object for the Camera.
methods.remarks: The #eye method is used to retrieve the eye Point3d object for the Camera.

methods.name: fov_is_height?
methods.summary: The #fov_is_height? method indicates whether the field of view is measured vertically, as opposed horizontally.
methods.remarks: The #fov_is_height? method indicates whether the field of view is measured vertically, as opposed horizontally.

methods.name: is_2d?
methods.summary: The #is_2d? method indicates whether the camera mode is two-point perspective or match photo mode, as opposed to a normal perspective or parallel projection camera.
methods.remarks: The #is_2d? method indicates whether the camera mode is two-point perspective or match photo mode, as opposed to a normal perspective or parallel projection camera.

methods.name: perspective?
methods.summary: The #perspective? method is used to determine whether a camera is a perspective or orthographic camera.
methods.remarks: The #perspective? method is used to determine whether a camera is a perspective or orthographic camera.

methods.name: scale_2d
methods.summary: The #scale_2d method returns a float indicating the scaling factor of 2 point perspective cameras.
methods.remarks: The #scale_2d method returns a float indicating the scaling factor of 2 point perspective cameras. When the camera is in two-point perspective and the user uses the zoom tools, this value will change. Zooming out will produce a value greater than 1.0.

methods.name: set
methods.summary: The #set method sets the camera orientation.
methods.remarks: The #set method sets the camera orientation. You have to set the camera eye, target and up parameters at the same time to make sure that you have a valid camera definition.

methods.name: target
methods.summary: The #target method retrieves Point3d that the camera is pointing at.
methods.remarks: The #target method retrieves Point3d that the camera is pointing at.

methods.name: up
methods.summary: The #up method is used to retrieve the up vector for the camera.
methods.remarks: The #up method is used to retrieve the up vector for the camera. This is

methods.name: xaxis
methods.summary: The #xaxis method is used to retrieve the x axis of the camera coordinate system.
methods.remarks: The #xaxis method is used to retrieve the x axis of the camera coordinate system. This value is computed from the cross product between the camera direction and the up vector.

methods.name: yaxis
methods.summary: The #yaxis method retrieves the y axis of the camera coordinate system.
methods.remarks: The #yaxis method retrieves the y axis of the camera coordinate system. This value is computed to be perpendicular the camera x and z axes. It is equivalent to the up direction, but is computed to make sure that it is perpendicular to the direction.

methods.name: zaxis
methods.summary: The #zaxis method retrieves the z axis of the camera coordinate system.
methods.remarks: The #zaxis method retrieves the z axis of the camera coordinate system. This value is computed. It is the same as Camera.direction

properties.name: aspect_ratio
properties.summary: The #aspect_ratio method is used to retrieve the aspect ratio of the Camera.
properties.remarks: The #aspect_ratio method is used to retrieve the aspect ratio of the Camera. `0.0` is the default value and denotes an aspect ratio matching that of the View.

properties.name: description
properties.summary: The #description method is used to retrieve the description for a Camera.
properties.remarks: The #description method is used to retrieve the description for a Camera.

properties.name: focal_length
properties.summary: The focal_length method is used to get the focal length in millimeters of perspective Camera.
properties.remarks: The focal_length method is used to get the focal length in millimeters of perspective Camera. This value is computed on the fly based on the camera #fov value and the camera #image_width value.

properties.name: fov
properties.summary: The #fov method retrieves the field of view of the Camera.
properties.remarks: The #fov method retrieves the field of view of the Camera. This is only applicable to perspective cameras.

properties.name: height
properties.summary: The #height method retrieves the height of a Camera.
properties.remarks: The #height method retrieves the height of a Camera. This is only valid if it is not a perspective camera.

properties.name: image_width
properties.summary: The #image_width method returns the width of the image, as used to calculate the #focal_length.
properties.remarks: The #image_width method returns the width of the image, as used to calculate the #focal_length. This value has no effect on how the view is displayed in SketchUp. The default value, 0, is interpreted by SketchUp as 36 mm (the width of traditional 35 mm film).

properties.name: perspective
properties.summary: The #perspective= method is used to set whether or not this is a perspective camera or an orthographic camera.
properties.remarks: The #perspective= method is used to set whether or not this is a perspective camera or an orthographic camera.
